good.text: Promises advance notice before terms change.
Says it does not sell your personal data.
You keep ownership and control of your content.

bad.text: Your personal data can be sold or shared with brokers.
The rules can change without telling you.
You give up your right to sue or join a class action.
They keep a broad license to content you create.
Your account can be closed at any time, for any reason.
You are tracked for third-party advertising.

clauses.label: Data Sale
clauses.summary: Anthropic may share your personal data with affiliates, service providers, and your organization for various business purposes, allowing broad third‑party access.
clauses.excerpt:

-   Affiliates & corporate partners: Anthropic discloses the categories of personal data described above between and among its affiliates and related entities.
-   Service providers & business partners: Anthropic may receive and disclose the categories of personal data described above with service providers and business partners for a variety of business purposes, including website and data

clauses.label: Data Sale
clauses.summary: The clause outlines multiple ways Anthropic can send your data to third‑party services, integrations, and legal authorities, giving them extensive rights to share your information.
clauses.excerpt:

clauses.label: Unilateral Changes
clauses.summary: Anthropic can change its service fees, and the new rates become effective either 30 days after they are posted or when you are notified.
clauses.excerpt:
## H. Fees

H.1. **Payment of Fees.** Customer is responsible for fees incurred by its account, at the rates specified on the [Model Pricing Page](https://www.anthropic.com/pricing), unless otherwise agreed by the parties. Anthropic may require prepayment for the Services in the form of credits or offer other types of credits, all of which are subject to Anthropic’s [Supplemental Credits Terms](https://www.anthropic.com/legal/credit-terms). clauses.label: Forced Arbitration
clauses.summary: Disputes must first go through a 45‑day informal process, after which either party can force arbitration and waive the right to a jury trial or class action.
clauses.excerpt:
## J. Disputes

J.1. **Disputes.** In the event of a dispute, claim or controversy relating to these Terms (“**Dispute**”), the parties will first attempt in good faith to informally resolve the matter. The party raising the Dispute must notify the other party (“**Dispute Notice**”). The other party will respond to the Dispute Notice in a timely manner. If the parties have not resolved the dispute within 45 days of delivery of the Dispute Notice, either party may seek to resolve the dispute through arbitration as stated in Section J.2 (Arbitration).

J.2. **Arbitration.** Any Dispute will be d

clauses.label: Forced Arbitration
clauses.summary: The agreement specifies mandatory arbitration locations and rules for EEA/UK customers and others, while still allowing parties to seek equitable relief.
clauses.excerpt:
## J. Disputes

J.2.a. For Customers residing in the EEA, Switzerland or UK, Disputes will be determined by a sole arbitrator in Dublin, Ireland pursuant the UNCITRAL Arbitration Rules as at present in force. The appointing authority shall be the President for the time being of the Law Society of Ireland.

J.2.b. For Customers residing anywhere else, Disputes will be determined by a sole arbitrator in San Francisco, CA pursuant to the Comprehensive Arbitration Rules and Procedures of Judicial Arbitration and Mediation Services, Inc.

clauses.label: Unilateral Changes
clauses.summary: Anthropic can change the terms at any time, with a 30‑day notice (or immediately for legal changes), and only written agreements signed by both parties are valid amendments.
clauses.excerpt:

M.3. **Amendment and Modification.** Anthropic may update these Terms at any time, to be effective 30 days after the updates are posted by Anthropic or Customer otherwise receives Notice, except that updates made in response to changes to law or regulation take effect immediately upon posting or Notice. Changes will not apply retroactively. No other amendment to or modification of these Terms is effective unless it is in writing and signed by both parties. clauses.summary: If you don’t pay the fees when due, Anthropic can suspend or terminate your access to the services and may pursue collection actions.

Customer is responsible for remitting any necessary withholding Taxes to the relevant authority on a timely basis and providing Anthropic with evidence of the same upon request. Where law provides for the reduction or elimination of withholding taxes, including via tax treaty, the parties will collaborate in good faith to do so. clauses.summary: Anthropic may suspend your access to all or part of the services for security, policy violations, legal reasons, or third‑party issues, and it isn’t liable for any resulting losses.
clauses.excerpt:

I.3.a. clauses.label: Third-Party Tracking
clauses.summary: The clause states that Anthropic uses cookies to collect information about users, personalize experiences, and market products, including sharing data with service providers.
clauses.excerpt:
## How Anthropic Uses Cookies

We use cookies to manage the Services and to collect information about you and your use of the Services. Cookies help us to recognize you, customize or personalize your experience, market additional products or services to you, and analyze the use of our Services to make it more useful to you. Cookies may also allow us to collect demographic and statistical data and provide this information to our service providers to help deliver their services.

We use cookies for the following purposes. You can also visit our [cookies table](https://support.anthropic.com/en/ar

clauses.summary: The clause explains that marketing cookies are used to target users and provide aggregated reporting, indicating data is used for advertising purposes.
clauses.excerpt:

Marketing

Cookies help us market our Services more effectively to users that we, our service providers, and our business partners believe may be interested in using our Services. They also help provide us with aggregated reporting and help us know when marketing content has been shown to you.

clauses.label: Notice Before Changes
clauses.summary: The company says it may update its cookie policy and will inform users of any material changes, encouraging them to review updates when they use the service.
clauses.excerpt:
## Changes to Our Cookie Policy

Anthropic may update this Cookie Policy from time to time. We will notify you of any material changes to this Cookie Policy, as appropriate, and update the Effective Date at the top of this page. We encourage you to review this page for updates when you access the Services.

clauses.label: No Data Sale
clauses.summary: Anthropic states it does not sell your personal data and lets you opt out of sharing data for targeted advertising, giving you control over that use.
clauses.excerpt:

-   Access & data portability: the right to request a copy of the personal data Anthropic processes about you, subject to certain exceptions and conditions. In certain cases and subject to applicable law, you have the right to port your information.

clauses.label: Notice Before Changes
clauses.summary: The clause says the privacy policy may be updated and the company will notify users of material changes and provide access to summaries and prior versions.
clauses.excerpt:
## 8\. Changes to Our Privacy Policy

Anthropic may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of any material changes to this Privacy Policy, as appropriate, and update the Effective Date at the top of [https://www.anthropic.com/legal/privacy](https://www.anthropic.com/legal/privacy). You can view a summary of privacy policy changes and previous versions in our [Privacy Center](https://privacy.anthropic.com/en/articles/10301952-updates-to-our-privacy-policy).

clauses.label: You Keep Your Content
clauses.summary: The clause says the customer keeps all rights to its inputs and owns the outputs, and the provider cannot use the content to train models.
clauses.excerpt:
## B. Customer Content

As between the parties and to the extent permitted by applicable law, Anthropic agrees that Customer (a) retains all rights to its Inputs, and (b) owns its Outputs. Anthropic disclaims any rights it receives to the Customer Content under these Terms. Subject to Customer’s compliance with these Terms, Anthropic hereby assigns to Customer its right, title and interest (if any) in and to Outputs. Anthropic may not train models on Customer Content from Services. “**Inputs**” means submissions to the Services by Customer or its Users and “**Outputs**” means responses generat

clauses.label: You Keep Your Content
clauses.summary: The clause states that, unless explicitly mentioned, neither party receives any rights to the other’s content or intellectual property, limiting any implied license.
clauses.excerpt:
## F. Intellectual Property

Except as expressly stated in these Terms, these Terms do not grant either party any rights to the other’s content or intellectual property, by implication or otherwise.

history.summary: Anthropic can now change its service fees at any time, with the new rates taking effect 30 days after posting or when the user is notified. The company also reserves the right to terminate or suspend user access to its services if fees are not paid on time, and may pursue collection actions. Additionally, users are no longer allowed to use the service to build competing products or train rival AI models. Users must also ensure their own users understand the limits of the outputs.

Anthropic updated its data sharing practices, now allowing the company to share personal data with affiliates, service providers, and other organizations for business purposes. This change may allow third parties to access user data. However, Anthropic also introduced more user-friendly elements, such as not selling personal data and giving users the option to opt out of targeted advertising. Users will also be notified of future policy changes and have access to summaries and prior versions.

The company updated its cookie policy to explicitly state that it uses cookies to collect user information for marketing and advertising purposes, including sharing data with third-party service providers. This means that user data may be used to target ads. The company also added a notice that it may update its cookie policy and will inform users of significant changes, allowing users to stay informed. No user-hostile changes were found beyond existing data collection and advertising practices.
